title: WULF Stock Climbs on $31 Target as TeraWulf Bets Big on Low-Carbon, Nuclear-Powered Bitcoin Mining
text: Cantor Fitzgerald has set a $31 price target for TeraWulf (NASDAQ: WULF), reflecting confidence in the company's low-carbon mining approach. The analyst rationale centers on several key factors. Energy Strategy Advantage: Cantor Fitzgerald highlighted that the company's energy strategy and infrastructure model help it grow long-term, particularly given increased ESG scrutiny in the sector. TeraWulf's Nautilus Cryptomine facility in Pennsylvania operates entirely on nuclear power sourced directly from the Susquehanna plant through a behind-the-meter agreement, positioning it as one of the first large-scale bitcoin mining sites in the world to use nuclear energy directly. The investment case benefits from three converging trends: rising demand for digital infrastructure (AI, cloud services, blockchain), increasing regulatory and investor pressure on emissions, and growing importance of stable, low-carbon baseload energy. As institutional investors increasingly factor emissions intensity into decisions, TeraWulf's access to predictable, low-carbon power provides operational stability and lower volatility compared to fossil fuel-dependent competitors.
